observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MDRRM Officer: 

Store the relief goods in a secure and well-ventilated room to deter the risk of possible loss and/or rapid spoilage; 

Prepare the RIS upon release of the relief goods to the MSWDO; 



Prepare the SSMI to be submitted to the GSO, supported with the RIS in numerical sequence; and 

Maintain an updated stock cards of all goods received from GSO. 

Assign custodial and recording functions of inventories to two different personnel to secure check and balance; and


Prepare a physical inventory report every end of the year and submit to GSO for consolidation. 

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant: 

Record the supplies procured by the Municipality in the appropriate inventory account upon purchase; maintain and update Supplies Ledger Cards for each inventory item; and 

Record the consumed or distributed supplies as expenses upon receipt of the SMMI from the GSO supported with the RIS and SSMI of the end-users.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management strictly comply with submission of perfected contracts, purchase orders and delivery documents including their supporting documents to COA within the reglementary periods prescribed under the relevant rules for the conduct of auditorial and legal reviews on pertinent transactions. According to the rules, these courses of actions will enable the auditor to immediately call the head of the agency regarding the defects and deficiencies noted in the contracts or purchase order who will suggest such corrective measures as are appropriated and warranted.
observations.managementAction: Copies of Purchase Orders and AIRs were not submitted to COA, except for some Infrastructure contracts but not on the prescribed time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to compute the total depreciation expenses that should be provided for each PPE item to bring the balances of the affected accounts in order

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:
prepare Journal Entry Vouchers to transfer to the General Fund the subject PPE items recorded under Trust Fund books; and
reclassify the completed projects still recorded as Construction in Progress to its appropriate asset accounts in the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
submit explanation to the Audit Team on the negative balances of the accounts Due to BIR, Due to Other Funds and Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable;
henceforth, exert best effort to immediately determine the cause of the negative balances so that necessary adjustments can be made in the books to put these accounts in order and to have them fairly presented in the financial statements; and
submit hardcopies of subsidiary ledgers to the Audit Team together with the submission of the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
submit justification to the Audit Team on why payment of ₱3,290,299.00 was made to the suppliers/contractors of the procured equipment even if these were not delivered.
Require the Inspectorate Team to submit explanation to the Audit Team why Inspection Report was made despite absence of delivery of the procured items.
Require the suppliers to refund immediately the amount paid for the undelivered items.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit justifications on why the ambulance intended for the transport of sick and injured persons/ LGU constituents to and from hospitals was converted into an ordinary service vehicle.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor restore the vehicle to its original state - an ambulance as programmed under the LGSF-FA or refund the amount to purchase an Ambulance loaded with the medical equipment and instruments.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other LGU Officials refund the amount received as payment for their travel expenses to Asturias, Cebu.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the suppliers of the goods procured as presented in Table 17 to post immediately a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent (1%) but not to exceed five percent (5%) of the total contract price, in order to protect not only the interest of the government but also those of the third parties involved in the execution of government projects, programs or activities. Otherwise, expenses or losses incurred due to the defective goods within warranty period be charged to the concerned officials approving payment.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Chairperson of the BAC to submit explanation to the Audit Team on awarding the projects to the contractors with erroneous NFCC as presented in Table 18.
We also recommend that henceforth, the Chairperson of the BAC verify the amounts presented in the computation of the contractors’ NFCC against valid supporting documents as required in RA 9184 in order to confirm its validity and correctly determine the eligibility of the contractor for award of contract.
Furthermore, we recommend that the BAC shall require the Contractors to submit to the Audit Team a valid justification on the exclusion in the computation of the NFCC the value of ongoing contracts as disclosed in the Statement of All Ongoing Government and Private Contracts.

We recommended that the:
RHU personnel, BHWs and other personnel hired by the municipality for COVID-19 isolation facility operations submit the lacking supporting documents/ records/ orders to the Audit Team to support payments of AHDP and SRA and for future similar transactions, observe compliance on the documentary requirements to avoid suspension in audit;
Municipal Accountant, henceforth, ensure that all necessary supporting documents are attached to these claims before forwarding these to the Municipal Mayor for approval; and
Cash Disbursing Officer require Authorization Letter/ Special Power of Attorney from persons who claim payments/ allowances other than the actual claimants.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman submit to the Audit Team justification why the posting requirement was not complied with and the required Mayor’s or Business Permit was not submitted and attached to the voucher.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit justification to the Audit Team why the vouchers were processed and endorsed for payment despite the lack of required documents.
We also recommended that the MSWD Officer submit the distribution lists for the relief goods distributed to recipients.
It is also recommended that henceforth, the Municipality should comply with the pertinent requirements before payment is made to avoid audit suspensions.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat and the GSO strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 96-010 in the submission to the Audit Team of Purchase Orders with complete supporting documents within the prescribed period for review and evaluation of procurement transactions.
We also recommended that the GSO carry out the receipt of delivered items and inform the Audit Team within twenty – four (24) hours from acceptance of the delivery as required in COA Circular No. 95-006 thru the submission of the Inspection and Acceptance Report to ascertain the propriety, validity and existence of the items prior to payment.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer strictly withhold the appropriate taxes from all payments made to the Municipality’s suppliers and service providers.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should:

Prepare a journal entry voucher to correct the erroneous recording on the following: 
Rechanneling of Waterway at Sitio Patag
Road Opening and Surfacing of Sitio Ipil II
Financial Assistance to ADM
Share from PCA
Balances from unidentified sources

Maintain an accurate and complete subsidiary ledger for each fund received by the Municipality and diligently update the same; 

Review thoroughly and judiciously the entries made by the bookkeeper before signing the Journal Entry Voucher; 

Recognize all unknown balances as income in the General fund if supported with cash.

Reconciliation was made already.

Prepare a supplemental LDRRMFIP for the PPAs on the STF-LDRRMF; and

 Coordinate with BAC and GSO in the preparation of detailed PPMP for all procurement to be submitted to BAC for consolidation to the APP. 

We also recommended that the BAC Chairman should: 

Orient and guide properly the MDRRMO in the preparation of PPMP; and

Prepare an APP based on the PPMP submitted. 

Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should check the accuracy of the amount in the OR issued by the supplier that should reconcile the amount of the check issued as well as in the disbursement vouchers.
 
Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant should: 

Submit justification why the vouchers were recommended for payment despite the lacking supporting documents; 

Submit the lacking documents noted with deficiencies within 5 days from receipt of this AOM; and

Henceforth, check thoroughly the completeness and veracity of all documents supporting the disbursement and require the submission of lacking documents prior to payment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive or his duly authorized official/s should submit to the Audit Team justifications on the above deficiencies. 

We also recommended that the General Services Officer should prepare the necessary records and reports in the inventory of donated items listed in Table 22.

Henceforth, the following activities to be performed by officials and employees concerned on related transactions: 

The General Service Officer shall: 

Prepare acknowledgement receipt in three copies: original to the donor; duplicate to the Accounting Unit and triplicate as File copy for donations received by the Municipality; 

Prepare issuance for in-kind donations issued and require the departments responsible for the distribution of the relief goods and other supplies to submit proof of receipt by and distribution to the recipients/end-users; 

Prepare Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances every month or every quarter of the Municipality is under quarantine for more than three months which shall be supported by Acknowledgement Receipt, Proof of Receipt by and Distribution to the Beneficiaries/end-users, and Inventory of remaining balances and submit the same to the NDRRMC through the OCD, copy furnished the respective COA auditors within ten (10) working days after the end of each quarter. 

Maintain Stock Cards/Property Card for all donations-in-kind received and update regularly; 

Maintain Registry of Donated Relief Goods for each kind of inventory item; and

Prepare PAR for issuance of the donated PPEs and ICS for issuances of tangible items which costs P15,000.00 or less and has a life of more than one year and are not considered as PPE to establish accountability over them and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the PAR and ICS. 

The Municipal Accountant shall: 

Summarize the data from the documents submitted by the General Services Officer according to the required disclosure, compare records with the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM to be maintained by the General Services Officer for check and balance, book-up all donated PPEs and disclose all the necessary information in the Notes to the Financial Statements. 

The Office of the Municipal Mayor should: 

Inform the General Services Officer whenever there are donations that the Municipality will be receiving for proper documentation, recording and reporting.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor restitute the loan amortization in CY 2020 from the savings of the 20% DF, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan. 

Moving forward, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Local Planning and Development Coordinator (LPDC) should include the payment of loan amortization in the 20% Development Fund and judiciously review all PPAs that are identified in all AIP from different fund so as not to overlook any item to be incorporated in the plan. 

We also recommended the Municipal Budget officer review thoroughly the contents of the annual/supplemental budget and its corresponding Investment program and make recommendations or changes on the noted deficiencies, if any. 

We further, recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify and inform the Municipal Budget Officer any disbursement made through Automatic Debit Arrangement (ADA) to insure availability of appropriation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor should issue an order for the Inventory Committee to
conduct physical count of all the properties of the Municipality and submit
the RCPPE as of December 31, 2020 on or before September 30, 2021, in
accordance with Sections 6.1 and 6.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006;
b. GSO, in coordination with the Accounting Department, should:
1. reconcile its property records with that of the RPCPPE in accordance
with the provisions of Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and
after completing the reconciliation procedures, the total reconciled
balances for each PPE account shall be established as the correct
balance of PPE at year-end; and
2. undergo disposal procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs, if there
are any, in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006;
c. Municipal Accountant should:
1. re-compute the depreciation of all depreciable assets and draw an
adjusting journal entry to set the accounts in order. Henceforth,
provide for the correct depreciation expense for the subject properties.
2. together with the GSO, prepare, maintain and update all the necessary
monitoring records and reports for PPEs as required in the IPSAS,
COA Circular No. 2020-006 and other standards.
3. record accounting entries for the recognition of PPEs found at station,
recognition of loss of PPEs, writing-off/dropping from the books of
accounts the remaining balances amounts lumped under the
unreconciled SLs of PPEs, and derecognition of non-existing/missing
items.
4. present the necessary disclosures on PPE in the Notes to the Financial
Statements.
5. Transfer PPEs in the Trust Fund to General Fund and provide
depreciation thereto.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant should:
a. prepare a journal entry voucher to correct the erroneous recording on
the following:
 Rechanneling of Waterway At Sitio Patag
 Road Opening and Surfacing of Sitio Ipil II
 Financial Assistance to ADM
 Share from PCA
 Balances from unidentified sources
b. maintain an accurate and complete subsidiary ledger for each fund
received by the Municipality and diligently update the same;
c. review thoroughly and judiciously the entries made by the bookkeeper
before signing the Journal Entry Voucher;
d. recognize all unknown balances as income in the General Fund if supported
with cash.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should:
a. record all supplies procured in the appropriate Inventory account upon
purchase;
b. record the consumed or distributed supplies as expenses upon receipt of the
RSMI from the GSO supported with the RIS; and
c. reconcile monthly with the records from the GSO to confirm the balances of
Inventories in its books with the balance on hand.

We also recommended that the General Service Officer should:
1. prepare Stock Card upon acceptance of the supplies;
2. issue RIS upon release of the supplies to the end-users;
3. submit monthly to the Municipal Accountant the consolidated RSMI duly
supported with the RIS; and
4. reconcile monthly with the Municipal Accountant to confirm balances on
hand.

We recommended that the General Services Officer should prepare the RSMI
duly supported with the RIS and acknowledgment receipt of the beneficiaries
and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant.

We also recommend that based on the RSMI submitted by the GSO, the
Municipal Accountant should record the issuances of inventories and submit a
copy of the JEV to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should:
a. prepare the Disbursement Voucher immediately to transfer the
unexpended balance of ₱920,346.69 to the appropriate account under the
Trust Fund in accordance with the provisions of the IRR of RA No. 10121
and COA Circular No. 2012-002 to safeguard the resources against risk
misuse/misappropriation;
b. revert back to the General Fund the unutilized LDRRMF after five (5) years
amounting ₱446,981.43 to which will be made available to fund other social
services;
c. reclassify the account used to record the previous years’ transfer from Due
to Other Funds to Trust Liabilities- DRRMF; and
d. regularly reconcile the record on LDRRM funds balance with the records
from the Municipal Budget Office.

1.12 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
1.12.1 Trace and verify immediately the reconciling items in the BRS,
especially those pertaining to prior years, and prepare the
necessary adjusting entries on all valid book to avoid its repetitive
inclusion in subsequent BRS;
1.12.2 prepare a written reminder or demand to the Municipal Treasurer
for the prompt submission of transactions documents and reports,
and record the dates of submission to avoid inclusion in possible
actions against non-rendition of accounts;
1.12.3 prepare and submit monthly the Bank Reconciliation Statements
for all LGU accounts; and
1.12.4 maintain an updated subsidiary ledger for each bank account of
the LGU.
1.13 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
1.13.1 Obtain every first week of the succeeding month the bank
statements and/or snapshot of all bank transactions of the previous
month together with the Debit/Credit Memos and submit the same
to the Municipal Accountant;
1.13.2 submit all collections and disbursement documents, with the
prescribed reports, to the Municipal Accountant on or before the
10th day of the month; and
1.13.3 retain proof of submission of transaction documents such as
transmittal letters or logbook, to avoid inclusion in possible actions
against non-rendition of accounts.

2.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the erroneous
entry made on the LGSF received and henceforth, coordinate with the
Municipal Treasurer for pertinent documents of the funds received to
ensure the proper recording and prepare and maintain a SL to record the
details of the funds.

3.14 We recommended that the Municipal Assessor update immediately the
assessment roll of real properties and furnish the same to the Municipal
Treasurer before the end of September 2020.
3.15 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer complete the
updating of the RPTAR in order to establish the amount due or
collectible for 2019 and prior years, and provide the same to the
Municipal Accountant before December 31, 2020 for recording of
RPT/SET Receivable.
3.16 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant record at the
beginning of the year the RPT/SET Receivables based on the RPTAR and
any adjustment thereon including tax arrears and penalties, if any.

4.12 We recommended that the MDRRM Officer MSWD Officer:
4.12.1 store the relief goods in a secure and well-ventilated room to
deter the risk of possible loss and/or rapid spoilage;
4.12.2 prepare the RIS upon release of the relief goods to the MSWDO;
4.12.3 prepare the SSMI to be submitted to the GSO, supported with
the RIS in numerical sequence; and
4.12.4 maintain an updated stock cards of all goods received from GSO.
4.12.5 assign custodial and recording functions of inventories to two
different personnel to ensure check and balance; and
4.12.6 prepare a physical inventory report every end of the year and
submit to GSO for consolidation.
4.13 We recommended that the MSWD Officer:
4.13.1 submit the Distribution List of relief goods to the GSO, duly
signed by each recipient, certified by the Punong Barangay and
with quantified list of the relief goods contained per pack;
4.13.2 maintain an updated Stock Cards of all goods received from
MDRRMO and GSO; and
4.13.3 assign custodial and recording functions of inventories to two
different personnel to ensure check and balance.
4.13.4 prepare a physical inventory report every end of the year and
submit to GSO for consolidation.
4.14 We also recommended that the GSO:
4.14.1 prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the SSMI every
month, duly supported with the RIS and SSMI from the
requisitioning offices;
4.14.2 maintain an updated Stock Cards of each goods procured by the
LGU;
4.14.3 assign custodial and recording functions of inventories to two
different personnel to ensure check and balance.
4.14.4 prepare a Physical Inventory Report on Supplies and Materials
in his custody at year-end for submission to the Accountant.
4.15 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
4.15.1 record the supplies procured by the Municipality in the
appropriate Inventory account upon purchase;
4.15.2 maintain and update Supplies Ledger Cards for each inventory
item; and
4.15.3 record the consumed or distributed supplies as expenses upon
receipt of the SSMI from the GSO supported with the RIS and
SSMI of the end-users.

5.17 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a.) prepare immediately the Disbursement Vouchers for the return of all
the unused funds to the National Government through the Bureau of
Treasury;
b.) verify thoroughly the nature and purpose of the funds received by the
LGU to avoid erroneous recording;
c.) maintain an accurate and complete subsidiary ledgers for each fund
received by the LGU and trace back to the source documents to arrive
at the correct details of the Due to NGA account.
5.18 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the LGU officials
charged with the implementation of PAPs funded by the National
Government shall obtain a copy of the MOA for every fund received and
furnish such copy to the Municipal Accountant as guide in determining the
purpose and other relevant conditions.
5.19 Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the
LGU officials concerned to submit to him a monthly status report on the
implementation of projects funded by the National Government and
monitor their compliance of the Memorandum of Agreement signed for
such purpose.

6.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Local
Development Council:
6.12.1 Identify specific projects to be funded from the 20%
Development Fund, including the use of continuing
appropriations, based on priority needs of the LGU and stop
lump sum appropriations;
6.12.2 Stop using the funds from other projects without the approved
authority for augmentations; and
6.12.3 Implement fully within the year all projects included in the
Annual Investment Program of the Municipality.

7.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local
Development Council, and the Municipal Planning and Development
Officer: (1) review and evaluate all projects funded from the Continuing
Appropriations of the 20% Development Fund; (2) re-appropriate the
funds for other necessary projects if the PPAs lined up in previous years
are no longer feasible; and (3) include in the AIP projects which are
capital or permanent in nature and responsive to the needs of the
constituents.
7.9 Citing that it is the Local Development Council who approves the PPAs under
the 20% Development Fund, the Municipal Engineer acknowledged the audit
recommendations and vowed to coordinate with the MPDO in the preparation
of the AIP to ensure compliance with the afore-cited regulations.

8.9 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant
maintain a list of completed projects as a reference for requiring contractors to
post the required warranty security.
8.10 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal
Engineer:
a. Issue only the Certificate of Acceptance one-year after completion of
the project or after the defects liability period; and
b. Require the contractor to post the warranty security upon the
issuance of Certificate of Acceptance; and
c. Ensure that the conditions provided in the Surety Bond issued by an
Insurance Company to replace the retention money is not
detrimental to the municipality.

9.7 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
a. Conduct detailed engineering on all infrastructure projects planned
by the LGU prior to its bidding; and
b. Implement and complete all projects within the period stipulated in
the contract.

10.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Budget Officer and the
MDRRM Officer retain in the Special Trust Fund the unutilized
LDRRMF for five years from the year it was appropriated.
10.10 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the MDRRM
Officer, include in the LDRRMFIP the projects, activities and program to
be funded from LDRRMF held in trust so that it can be utilized
simultaneous with current appropriations without reverting it back to the
General Fund before the lapse of the 5-years requirement.
10.11 In a letter reply to the AOM, the LDRRM Officer commented that the
reversion of LDRRMF in the amount of ₱12.6 Million to the General Fund
was made through the initiative of the previous Municipal Budget Officer.
His office submitted a presentation to the MBO hinting revision of the
LDRRMF Investment Plan detailing the various PPAs from the previous years
but was not given any consideration. However, he vowed that all
recommendations will be noted and ensure their considerations in the future
LDRRMF transactions especially on the inclusion to the current LDRRMFIP
the proposed utilization of LDRRMF in the Special Trust Fund.

11.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the MDRRMO consult
and discuss with the Municipality’s DRRM Council the contents of the
Municipality’s LDRRMFIP as to the necessity and continuity of the
projects/programs/activities that are listed therein to avoid modifications
during the year which are unauthorized.

12.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
5.) Issue a directive to submit to the Municipal Accountant before
December 31, 2020 their Liquidation Reports or refund the amount of
their cash advance;
6.) Stop the grant of additional cash advances unless the previous cash
advances are fully settled;
7.) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a quarterly report on
employees with unliquidated cash advances for issuance of Demand
Letters, and enforce salary deductions if left unheeded; and
8.) Initiate the preparation of a request for write-off on dormant receivables
which are found uncollectible, after exhausting all possible remedies.

13.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) create a Committee which will review the Market Code of the
Municipality, update the rates of taxes and fees and if warranted, make
any revision to attain more efficient administration and collection
system in operating the Merida Public Market to maximize the income
generation capabilities of the LGU;
b) recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan for approval any proposed
revisions on the Tax Ordinance;
c) require the Municipal Treasurer to draft a Contract of Lease in
accordance with the approved revisions in the LGU’s Tax Ordinance;
and
d) sign a Contract of Lease with each stallholder upon their application as
basis for the collection of rentals.
13.9 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit
a list of actual occupants of the market stall to the Municipal Mayor as
basis in the preparation of lease contracts.
13.10 The Municipal Treasurer commented that the appointment of a Market
Supervisor-Designate is in process. The latter will oversee the operations in
the public market based on the newly approved Municipal Tax Ordinance
which will be revised by the officials concerned.

We recommend that the General Services Officer maintain property cards, and the Municipal Accountant maintain property, plant and equipment ledger cards, reconcile the balances of the two (2) records and adjust any discrepancy noted.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the donated equipment by securing from the donor agency the Deed of Donation and related documents and record the cost of the said equipment both in the GSO and Accountant’s records/books

observations.reason: The accounting office is just waiting for the actual inventory and appraisal of existing PPE’s for us to be able to prepare necessary adjusting entries to reflect the appropriate amounts.

Copies of the Memorandum of Agreement and Work and Financial Plan are not furnished to the Audit Team.

Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the Local Development Council implement fully during the year the programs and projects listed in the Annual Investment Plan in order to deliver to the intended beneficiaries the benefits that the program offers.  Also, to include only in the AIP activities that partake the nature of capital expenditures.
     
      Likewise, we re-iterate the recommendation that the MSWDO ensures that the counterpart funds of the municipality to KALAHI-CIDDS should be utilized only for the capital expenditures and not for the operating expenses.
observations.reason: The 20% DF was not fully utilized. There are still expenses that are not in accordance with the guidelines on the said funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mandate the MDRRMO, the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to prepare and submit the required reports within the period provided by regulations to facilitate the review and validation on the use of LDRRMF, otherwise impose the sanctions provided for by law.
observations.reason:
LDRRMF Investment Plan and the Report on Sources and Utilization
were not  submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to submit the above-cited transaction documents and financial reports within five (5) days from receipt hereof.  

     Also, subsequent submission of transaction accounts and financial reports should strictly be in accordance with the timeline provided in the regulations to avoid the imposition of penalties on non-rendition of accounts.  This recommendation is without prejudice to the result of post audit of DVs and revenue accounts.
observations.reason: Submission of the transaction documents were not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor maximize the use of the 20% Development Fund for the implementation of eligible projects in order to provide the desired benefits of the development projects to the constituents.  

Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant submits explanation for the use of 20% Development Fund for programs and activities that are not in line with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.  Subsequent utilization of the 20% DF should be strictly in accordance with the guidelines on the use of the said fund. 

Moreover, the MSWD Officer ensure that the counterpart fund for KALAHI-CIDDS programs is utilized for capital expenditures and not for operating expenses.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRM Officer to implement fully the activities in the LDRRMIP in order to utilize the mitigation fund and Trust Fund of the LDRRMF in accordance with the prescribed guidelines to fully equip the LGU and its constituents in the preparation and management of disaster risks towards a resilient community in times of calamities.

the PPA.s
in the LDRRMP
to prepare
the LGU in the
management
of disaster
and in responding
to emergencies

Just assumed office on the last quarter of 2016 with very limited understanding on the actual implementation of the PPA’s 
as incorporated on the LDRRMP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, and the designated program and project coordinators, strengthen coordination with source agencies and ensure proper and timely implementation of projects and programs, closely monitor the projects, from inception to completion, for transparency purposes.

Also, secure from the funding agencies duly signed copies of the MOA for every project funded by such agency as soon as the corresponding fund is received, and furnish the same to the audit team.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented.

The implementations of some projects were not executed as planned because management encountered problems in the procurement process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend and the Municipal Mayor agreed that, through the MENRO, his administration will provide the necessary support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own Materials Recovery Facility and implement waste segregation.  

Likewise, they would intensify the information dissemination campaign to adopt measures toward effective solid waste management at the barangay level and would take efforts to stop the operation of its open dumpsite and find alternative ways to manage solid wastes that will ensure adequate protection on the environment and the general health conditions of the constituents of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management is already planning to purchase a lot for the sanitary landfill.
observations.reason:
The Municipality is not practicing the proper segregation of solid waste.




Not Implemented

They did not have the information campaign on proper solid waste management.

The Municipality is still operating in an open dump site

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record completed projects
      to the appropriate account in the PPE upon its completion,  regardless
      if paid or not,  based  on  the  quarterly  report  of  the  Municipal
      Engineer.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the  erroneous
      entry made and henceforth, coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and
      from  the  Municipal  Engineer  to  secure  any  supporting  document.
      Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer should secure  supporting  documents
      for any funds received to ascertain the  proper  utilization  of  such
      funds.

We recommended that the designated Custodians of  each  requisitioning
      office:

     1. Prepare the RIS when supplies are issued to end-users/patients;
     2. Prepare, and submit to the GSO, the SSMI every 5th day of the month,
        duly supported with the corresponding RIS in numerical sequence;
     3. Maintain an updated stock card;
     4. Conduct physical count of inventory items by type every June 30  and
        December 31 every year; and
     5. Prepare, and submit to the GSO, the RPCPI every 15th day of July and
        January each year.

  We also recommended that the GSO:

     6. Prepare and submit Municipal Accountant the consolidated SSMI  every
        15th day of the month, duly supported with the RIS and SSMI from the
        requisitioning offices, and;
     7. Prepare the consolidated RPCI and submit to the Municipal Accountant
        and Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each  year  for
        the first and second semesters, respectively, to confirm balances on
        hand

  Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

     8. Record the supplies procured by the Municipality in the  appropriate
        Inventory account upon purchase;
     9. Record the consumed or distributed supplies as expenses upon receipt
        of the consolidated SSMI from the GSO supported  with  the  RIS  and
        SSMI of the requisitioning office or end-users; and
    10. Reconcile the semestral  RPCI  of  the  GSO  with  the  balances  of
        Inventories in its books.

We recommended that  the  MDRRMO  prepare  the  LDRRMFIP  annually  to
      support  the  budget  for  LDRRMF  and  the  Report  on  Sources   and
      Utilization of DRRMF to account for the funds and submit the  same  to
      the Audit Team on or before the 15th of the month.  This   is  without
      prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension, if warranted.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

      1. Record in the Trust Fund books, using the Trust Liabilities- DRRMF
         account, the transfers of the unexpended  QRF  and  the  DRRM-MOOE
         funds;
      2. Revert back to the General Fund the unutilized LDRRMF  after  five
         (5) years which will  be  made  available  to  fund  other  social
         services;
   3. Prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers;

We also recommended that the  Municipal  Budget  Officer  prepare  and
      maintain separate registry for LDRRMF –QRF, MOOE and CO.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  facilitate  the  early
      transfer of Certificates of Titles in favor of the Municipality and to
      include in the LGU’s budget the necessary funds for the said transfer.

We also recommended that the Municipal Assessor review  the  valuation
      of the lots owned by the LGU  and  provide  the  GSO  with  sufficient
      information about the lot such as to the location and measurement.

We recommended the following corrective measures:

     1. The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal  Accountant  with  the
        complete description and cost segregation  of  road  components  to
        facilitate the preparation  of  the  subsidiary  ledgers  for  such
        account;


     2. The Municipal Accountant reclassify the affected accounts,  prepare
        the necessary ledgers, schedules and reports as called for  in  the
        afore-cited Circular;

     3. The GSO update the Report of the Local  Road  Network,  maintain  a
        Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a  complete  Local  Road
        Network Property Card for all roads and its components;

     4. The Inventory Committee conduct a physical count on  all  municipal
        roads of the Municipality to validate their existence, and;


     5. All offices concerned to reconcile their records inorder to come-up
        with an accurate and reliable balances and reports.

We recommended that:

     1) the Municipal Development Council evaluate  meticulously  the  PPAs
        listed in 20% DF Annual Investment Plan; and


     2) the Municipal Mayor ensure the full utilization of the 20%  DF  for
        the implementation of eligible projects in  order  to  provide  its
        constituents the intended benefits of the development projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculture Officer:

     1)  Follow  all  conditions  in  the  MOA  to  avoid  possible   legal
        impediments in the implementation of the program; and


     2) Implement and complete within the prescribed timeframe all projects
        and activities of the YRRP.

We recommended that the Chairman of the BAC and its Technical  Working
      Group follow strictly the provisions of RA  9184  in  its  procurement
      activities and support all contract agreements with its integral  parts
      and other necessary information before  submitting  the  same  to  the
      Audit Team.  This observation is without prejudice to the issuance  of
      the Notices of Suspension, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  ensure  that  all  plans  and
      designs for the construction of agricultural structures and facilities
      to be undertaken by  the  LGU  are  reviewed  and  signed  by  a  duly
      registered  agricultural  engineer  to   ascertain   conformity   with
      agricultural engineering standards.

We recommended that the SWM Board convene and perform their  functions
      as mandated in RA 9003 to ensure that the SWM Plan is carried out  and
      give alternative solutions to identified deficiencies relative to  the
      implementation of the said Plan.

We also recommended the  MENRO-Designate,  in  coordination  with  the
      General Services  Officer,  implement  the  SWM  Plan,  document  such
      activity for submission to the SWM Board for  reference  in  decision-
      making.

Lastly, we  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  appropriate  the
      necessary funds to finance the effective SWM activities including  the
      procurement of the necessary equipment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant compute the amount due to each barangay for their share in the RPT taxes collected by the LGU, and prepare the Disbursement Voucher for the release of such barangay share within five (5) days after the end of each quarter.

      We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer release all barangay shares from RPT to the barangays concerned.

We recommend that the Human Resource and Management Officer reconcile their records with the GSIS and communicate immediately to the said agency any necessary change in the employees’ employment status to update the records of the GSIS and tally with the LGU records on premium contributions.
     
     We also recommend that the Agency Authorized Officer (AO) furnish monthly all payroll preparer with copies of the GSIS billing to ensure that the correct amount of monthly premium contributions and loan amortizations deducted in the payroll are based on the said GSIS billing.  

     Lastly, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant reconcile monthly the actual deductions in the payroll against the GSIS Billing and against the amount remitted to avoid discrepancies therein.

We recommend that the Local School Board Council incorporate in its budget, programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care Development to ensure a more strategic and efficient utilization of the funds.

We recommend that the MCPC perform actively their functions and submit the Annual Work and Financial Plan, Local Plan Development for Children (LPDC) and Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC) indicating therein the budgeted amount to undertake the activities for the protection and welfare of the children in the municipality. Also, submit pertinent reports to evaluate the implementation of the PPAs and the utilization of the funds.

We also recommend that the MCPC coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer so that the annual budget of the Municipality will include an appropriation equivalent to 1% of its IRA for the MCPC activities.
observations.reason: For FY 2019, 1% LCPC Fund was fully provided and Annual Work and Financial Plan were also prepared.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee and direct its members to conduct a complete physical count of all properties of the LGU and render a report thereon through an RPCPPE. 

Also, require the General Services Officer to maintain stock cards and property cards, and

The Municipal Accountant to maintain property, plant and equipment ledger cards as basis for the reconciliation with the RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of records and actual count of properties are being done.
observations.reason: The balance per RPCPPE and accounting records are not reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly remit all amount due to the BIR and within the reglementary period.   Also, the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in identifying the unremitted balances of prior years and update the accounting records by maintaining separate subsidiary ledgers for each type of taxes withheld to facilitate the timely and accurate remittance of the same to the BIR.
observations.reason:
There are still a minimal amount of taxes withheld that are not remitted within the prescribed period.
Prior years error can no longer be identified due to unavailability of necessary documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend for management to strengthen the operation of preventive and monitoring controls to ensure timely submission of liquidation reports and strictly cause the settlement or liquidation of cash advances to protect the concerned against the legal consequences of committing said deficiency. 

We further recommend:

Liquidation of all cash advances shall be made immediately after the purposes for which they were granted have been served. We recommend to the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts to demand for the liquidation of outstanding cash advances from the concerned accountable officers and employees.  Resort to legal action if evidences warrant. 

  Refrain from granting additional cash advances to Accountable Officers with unsettled cash advances.

 Stop sending Job Order workers on trainings, conferences and seminars and other official travels. Henceforth, only government officials and employees will be granted for the payment of travelling expenses and registration of trainings, seminars and conferences, in accordance with laws and regulations.

We recommend that management direct the accounting section to reconcile the accounts and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances for a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: For current year, we make sure that remitted amounts match with the amounts withheld
observations.reason: Negative balances can no longer be identified due to unavailability of documents necessary for the investigation of such negative balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should strive to retrieve accounting and bank records, reports and documents as well as pertinent municipal accounting records and files to be able to identify the variance reflected in the submitted Bank Reconciliation Statements.  Thereafter, draw the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to take up the recording of these reconciling items in the books and provide a copy to the depository bank to correct their own records on the municipality’s bank account.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments are made on the identified reconciling items.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the following courses of actions be undertaken:

The Accounting Office shall be strictly directed to record expenses using the correct account title and code, otherwise, if continued, the use of the “Other MOE” may be considered intentional and for purposes not in conformity with the mandated honest as well as objective recording of transactions.  Such course of action shall be adhered to for the agency’s financial statements to be considered reliable sources of financial information.

In the preparation of the Annual and Supplemental Budgets of the municipality, we recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer should strive to see to it that that Budgets should be presented in such detail that proposed Programs and Projects to be undertaken by each department or office will reflect the estimated/projected type of expenditure to be incurred during implementation and not in lump sum amount.

We recommended that the management strictly comply with submission of perfected contracts, purchase orders and delivery documents including their supporting documents to COA within the reglementary periods prescribed under the relevant rules for the conduct of auditorial and legal reviews on pertinent transactions. According to the rules, these courses of actions will enable the auditor to immediately call the head of the agency regarding defects and deficiencies noted in the contracts or purchase orders who will suggest such corrective measures as are appropriate and warranted.
observations.managementAction:
Said documents were prepared and keep by the Gen.
Service Office (GSO) and this office will try to assist the GSO to comply with the said requirement.
observations.reason:
Contracts on bidded infrastructure projects and supplies are submitted to the Audit Team but not within the prescribed period. While the
Purchase Orders (PO), Acceptance and Inspection Reports are not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
1.) coordinate with each depository bank of the LGU for the early receipt of
monthly bank statements or snapshot to facilitate the timely preparation
of bank reconciliation statements;
2.) prepare the necessary adjusting entries on all valid reconciling items in
the BRS;
3.) reconcile accounting records with Treasurer’s cashbook at least every
quarter and make the necessary corrections on errors detected;
4.) close dormant accounts under the Trust Fund and return to the source
agencies any excess amount. If no longer traceable, transfer such funds
to regular operating funds as additional income.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a separate
cashbook for LDRRMF and DAR-ARCP II (LGU Equity) accounts.

We recommended that the General Services Officer maintain property cards,
and the Municipal Accountant maintain property, plant and equipment ledger
cards, reconcile the balances of the two (2) records and adjust any discrepancy
noted.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the donated
equipment by securing from the donor agency the Deed of Donation and related
documents and record the cost of the said equipment both in the GSO and
Accountant’s records/books

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant perform the following
actions:
a) Determine the conditions of the dormant accounts and validate the
existence of the conditions by obtaining the applicable documents as
enumerated in Section 8.3b of COA Circular No. 2016-005;
b) Prepare the Schedule of Dormant Accounts by Accountable Officer
/debtor/government entity and by account, certify its correctness and
have it approved by the Head of the government entity;
c) Prepare the request for write-off of the receivable accounts and submit
to the Municipal Mayor, with the necessary supporting document, for
approval and submission to COA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor
to:
1. update continuously the Assessment Rolls as to the conditions of the real
properties, identify the exact addresses of declared owners of such
properties, the exemptions on RPT, if any;
2. furnish the Municipal Treasurer with the Assessment Rolls within the
prescribed period;
3. prepare a written request to the Provincial Official concerned on the
general revision of market value of the real properties in the
Municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the RPT/SET
Receivable at the beginning of each year based on the Assessment Rolls from the
Municipal Assessor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the focal persons of the
programs/projects to:
1. coordinate with each source agency and obtain copies of the
Memorandum of Agreement and Work and Financial Plan and furnish
the same to the Municipal Accountant and the Audit Team;
2. complete the programs and projects assigned to them so that full
liquidation can be submitted;
3. coordinate with the Municipal Accountant so that the corresponding
liquidation reports will be prepared and submitted to the source agencies.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer determine the
availability of cash of all funds received from NGAs, especially the balances of
completed programs/projects and those which has not been fully liquidated in the
books of the LGU and issue the check in favor of the source agencies to return all
remaining balances and unutilized amount.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Local Development
Council implement fully during the year the programs and projects listed in the
Annual Investment Plan in order to deliver to the intended beneficiaries the benefits
that the program offers. Also, to include only in the AIP activities that partake the
nature of capital expenditures.

Likewise, we re-iterated the recommendation that the MSWDO ensures that
the counterpart funds of the municipality to KALAHI-CIDDS should be utilized
only for the capital expenditures and not for the operating expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute the amount due
to each barangay for their share in the RPT taxes collected by the LGU, and
prepare the Disbursement Voucher for such barangay share while the Municipal
Treasurer issue the checks for its release within five (5) days after the end of each
quarter.

We recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer
reconcile their records with the GSIS and communicate immediately to the said
agency any necessary change in the employees’ employment status to update the
records of the GSIS and tally with the LGU records on premium contributions.

We also recommended that the Agency Authorized Officer (AO) furnish
monthly all payroll preparer with copies of the GSIS billing to ensure that the
correct amount of monthly premium contributions and loan amortizations deducted
in the payroll are based on the said GSIS billing.

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile monthly
the actual deductions in the payroll against the GSIS Billing and against the amount
remitted to avoid discrepancies therein.

We recommended that the Local School Board Council incorporate in its
budget, programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care Development to
ensure a more strategic and efficient utilization of the funds.

We recommended that the MCPC perform actively their functions and submit
the Annual Work and Financial Plan and other reports indicating therein the
budgeted amount to undertake the activities for the protection and welfare of the
children in the municipality. Also, submit the accomplishment reports to evaluate
the implementation of the PPAs and the related expenses incurred.

We also recommended that the MCPC coordinate with the Municipal Budget
Officer so that the budget of the MCPC activities in the AWFP will be equivalent to
1% of its annual IRA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mandate the MDRRMO, the
Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to prepare and submit the required
reports within the period provided by regulations to facilitate the review and
validation on the use of LDRRMF, otherwise impose the sanctions provided for by
law.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the above-cited transaction
documents and financial reports immediately to avoid the imposition of penalties on
non-rendition of accounts. This recommendation is without prejudice to the result
of post audit of DVs and revenue accounts.

Also, subsequent submission of transaction accounts and financial reports
should strictly be in accordance with the timeline provided in the regulations.

observations.reason: Lack of personnel turnover
There is no existing regulation on the use of the KALAHI- CIDDS – LGU Counterpart Fund.
Lack of personnel
Some projects were not implemented as planned; problems in procurement process.
There are still unremitted balances.
Failure in the procureme nt process.
There are plans and partial actions; some implementatio n halted due to Typhoon Yolanda.
Although efforts were made, some unliquidated advances could not be located; deceased personnel records no longer available.
There was no proper turn-over of accounting records and files between outgoing and incoming accountants.
Although efforts are being done, problems persist; some recommendations are not yet fully implemented.
There are still unresolved reconciling items from prior years as there was no proper submission of documents and turn-over; some adjustments made but prior year items remain.
There are still expenditures charged to Other MOE although some adjustments were made; need for detailed budgeting.
The P200,000 was not reverted to unappropriated surplus as required by Supreme Court decision.
Some contracts and supporting documents were submitted but not within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  create  an  Inventory
Committee and direct its members to conduct a  complete  physical  count  of
all properties of the LGU and render a report  thereon  through  an  RPCPPE.
Also, require the General Services  Officer  to  maintain  stock  cards  and
property cards, and the Municipal Accountant  to  maintain  property,  plant
and equipment ledger cards as basis for the reconciliation with the RPCPPE.

We recommend that the Municipal  Accountant  prepare  and  submit  the
Statement of Financial Performance under Trust Fund together with all  other
required financial statements, as required under  Philippine  Public  Sector
Accounting Standards (PPSAS) No. 1, within the prescribed period.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor maximize  the  use  of  the  20%
Development Fund for the implementation of eligible  projects  in  order  to
provide  the  desired  benefits  of  the   development   projects   to   the
constituents.


      Likewise, we recommend that  the  Municipal  Budget  Officer  and  the
Municipal Accountant submits explanation for  the  use  of  20%  Development
Fund for programs and activities that are not in line  with  DILG-DBM  Joint
Memorandum  Circular  No.  2011-1  dated   April   13,   2011.    Subsequent
utilization of the  20%  DF  should  be  strictly  in  accordance  with  the
guidelines on the use of the said fund.


      Moreover, the MSWD Officer ensure that the counterpart fund for KALAHI-
CIDDS programs is utilized for capital expenditures and  not  for  operating
expenses.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the  MDRRM  Officer  to
implement fully the activities in  the  LDRRMIP  in  order  to  utilize  the
mitigation fund and  Trust  Fund  of  the  LDRRMF  in  accordance  with  the
prescribed guidelines to fully equip the LGU and  its  constituents  in  the
preparation and management of disaster risks towards a  resilient  community
in times of calamities.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive,  through  the  designated
program  and  project  coordinators,  strengthen  coordination  with  source
agencies and  ensure  proper  and  timely  implementation  of  projects  and
programs.   Moreover,  closely  monitor  the  projects,  from  inception  to
completion, for transparency purposes.


      Also, we recommend that the concerned offices secure from the  funding
agencies duly signed copies of the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)  for  every
project funded  by  such  agency  as  soon  as  the  corresponding  fund  is
received, and furnish the same to the audit team.

We recommend that the Local School Board, through the Municipal  Mayor
as Chairman, appropriate and utilize the  Special  Education  Fund  strictly
for programs, projects and activities that are explicitly authorized in  the
guidelines for the use of the  SEF.   Also,  give  utmost  priority  on  the
improvement of school facilities and acquisition of books and  instructional
materials to get the maximum benefits offered by the fund.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the  Municipal  Treasurer
to strictly remit all amounts due to the BIR  and  within  the  reglementary
period.   Also, the Municipal Accountant to  exert  efforts  in  identifying
the unremitted balances of prior years and update the accounting records  by
maintaining separate subsidiary ledgers for each type of taxes  withheld  to
facilitate the timely and accurate remittance of the same to the BIR.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to  conduct  the
bidding of the needed goods of the agency in bulk on a  quarterly  basis  in
accordance with the approved APP.   Procure common-use supplies through  the
PS-DBM  and  if  not  available,  obtain  the  certification  as   to   non-
availability of stocks.


      We also recommend that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  all  Heads  of
Departments/Offices to stop submitting requisitions on  piecemeal  and  base
their  requests  on  the  approved  APP  as  to  the  period  and  mode   of
procurement.

We recommend and the Municipal Mayor agreed that, through the  MENRO,
his administration will provide  the  necessary  support  in  enjoining  the
barangays to establish their own Materials Recovery Facility  and  implement
waste  segregation.   Likewise,  they  would   intensify   the   information
dissemination campaign  to  adopt  measures  toward  effective  solid  waste
management at the  barangay  level  and  would  take  efforts  to  stop  the
operation of its open dumpsite and find alternative  ways  to  manage  solid
wastes that will ensure adequate  protection  on  the  environment  and the
general health conditions of the constituents of the Municipality.

We recommend
that contracts
of job order
workers should
contain
statements of
duties and
responsibilitie
s in order to
serve as
standard for
which
assessment on
the
effectiveness,
efficiency and
necessity of
their services
as well as
economy in
government
operation can
be determined.

We recommend
that:

a. General
Services Office
or the
Municipal
Engineer Office
who has the
custody of the
municipality’s
motor vehicles
will be
required to
issue duly
approved and
properly
accomplished
Driver’s Trip
Ticket serially
numbered to
support the
Monthly Report
of Official
Travels.

b. In addition,
a Monthly
Report of Fuel
Consumption of
government
motor
transportation
will be
prepared and
submitted.
These are to be
duly adhered
for management
to make
possible the
proper
monitoring of
the agency’s
consumption of
fuels.

c. Strict
adherence to
the required
submission of
properly filled
up and complete
documents as
proof that fuel
procured and
issued are
necessary,
appropriate and
valid pursuant
to relevant
rules.

We recommend
for management
that:

a. Liquidation
of all cash
advances shall
be made
immediately
after the
purposes for
which they were
granted have
been served. We
recommend to
the Municipal
Accountant to
exert more
efforts to
demand for the
liquidation of
outstanding
cash advances to
the concerned
accountable
officers and
employees.
Resort to legal
action if
evidences
warrant.

b. Refrain from
granting
additional cash
advances to
Accountable
Officers with
unsettled cash
advances.

c. Stop sending
Job Order
workers on
trainings,
conferences and
seminars and
other official
travels. Henceforth,
only government
officials and
employees will
be granted for
the payment of
travelling
expenses and
registration of
trainings,
seminars and
conferences, in
accordance with
laws and
regulations.
observations.reason:
There are
still cash
advances
from prior
years
remained
unliquidated.
Cash
advance are
not granted
unless
previous one
is already
liquidated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that
complete
documentation
should be
strictly adhered
for all
disbursements
paid to
establish
validity,
propriety and
legality of the
transaction.

We further
recommend that
the management
will exercise
prudence and
frugality in the
spending of
government
funds to ensure
efficiency,
economic and
effectiveness in
the operation of
the agency.

In view of the
foregoing, we
recommend that
management
should, 1) assign
Mr. Nodalo to
other function
not related with
collections, 2)
tighten-up
internal
control
measures on the
remittance of
collections, 3)
liquidating
officer/treasur
er should
strictly monitor
and be vigilant
enough to
detect
unremitted
collections
from collectors
to avoid this
kind of
circumstances
in the future.

We recommend for
strict
compliance in
the submission
of Utilization
Reports to
pertinent offices
and the COA, in
effect, capacitate
the agency to
present
outright
information
regarding the
MDRRMF and make
possible the
assessment thru
audit the
propriety as
well as validity
of disbursements
made out of the
fund.

We recommend
that management
implement GAD
thru the
adherence to
prescribed
processes
initially thru
the provision of
budgetary
allocations at
the prescribed
amount as well
as these
courses of
actions: a) require
the GAD Focal
Point/Person to
submit the
agency’s GAD
Plan to the
Department of
Budget and
Management
(DBM) and
National
Commission On
the Role of
Filipino Women
(NCFRW) for
review and
approval to
ensure that
programs/subjec
ts/activities
reflected
thereat are in
accordance with
the existing
regulations and
guidelines; and
b) the GAD
Focal
Point/Person
shall furnish
the Audit Team
, NCRFW and
other
regulating
offices the GAD
Plan and Budget
and
Accomplishment
Reports for
monitoring
purposes.

In order to
prevent the
conclusion of
irregular as
well as invalid
performance of
duties
concerning
submission of
accounts and
to avoid
penalties
provided for the
contradictions
of relevant
laws and rules,
the
implementation
of hereunder
recommendations
is proposed,
viz:

Instruct the
accountant to
immediately
submit all
accounts to the
Audit Team as
specified by
laws and
regulations;
Instruct the
treasurer and
all accountable
officers to
practice the
submission of
accounts to the
Accounting
Office within
the
reglementary
period, the
same to be
covered with
transmittal
letters as
proof thereof.
